PAT-56482EA1.name: Patient 2EA1
PAT-56482EA1.medical_history:
# Patient Clinical Record

**Patient ID:** AUTO-GENERATED
**Name:** Robert Chen
**Age:** 68
**Gender:** Male

## Chief Complaint
Progressive difficulty walking and balance issues over the past 3 months.

## Medical History
- Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (diagnosed 2015, A1C: 7.2%)
- Hypertension (controlled with Lisinopril 10mg daily)
- Peripheral neuropathy in both feet
- Former smoker (quit 10 years ago)

## Current Medications
- Metformin 1000mg twice daily
- Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Gabapentin 300mg three times daily

## Physical Examination
- Height: 5'10" | Weight: 185 lbs | BMI: 26.5
- Blood pressure: 128/82 mmHg
- Reduced sensation in bilateral feet (monofilament test positive)
- Mild weakness in ankle dorsiflexion bilaterally

## Gait Assessment
- Stride Length: 0.58 m
- Gait Speed: 0.92 m/s
- Symmetry Index: 0.82
- Wide-based gait observed
- Uses walls for support when walking

## Recent Falls
- 1 fall in past 6 months (no injury)
- Near-falls reported weekly

## Patient Goals
Maintain independence and continue daily walks in the neighborhood.

PAT-90FA1DBF.name: Patient 1DBF
PAT-90FA1DBF.medical_history:
# Patient Clinical Record

**Patient ID:** AUTO-GENERATED
**Name:** James Morrison
**Age:** 71
**Gender:** Male

## Chief Complaint
Tremor in right hand and slowness of movement noticed by wife over past year.

## Medical History
- Parkinson's Disease (diagnosed 6 months ago, Hoehn & Yahr Stage 2)
- Benign prostatic hyperplasia
- Depression (mild, treated)
- No prior falls

## Current Medications
- Carbidopa/Levodopa 25/100mg three times daily
- Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily
- Sertraline 50mg daily

## Physical Examination
- Height: 5'11" | Weight: 175 lbs | BMI: 24.4
- Resting tremor right hand (4-6 Hz)
- Cogwheel rigidity bilateral upper extremities
- Masked facies
- Reduced arm swing with walking

## Gait Assessment
- Stride Length: 0.52 m
- Gait Speed: 0.78 m/s
- Symmetry Index: 0.75
- Festinating gait
- Difficulty initiating movement
- Freezing episodes reported (2-3 times weekly)

## Recent Falls
- No falls in past year
- 2 near-falls during freezing episodes

## Functional Status
- Independent in ADLs with extra time
- Still driving (restricted to daytime, familiar routes)
- Retired engineer, cognitively intact

## Treatment Goals
Maintain mobility and prevent falls as disease progresses.

PAT-C9F6F2C7.name: Patient F2C7
PAT-C9F6F2C7.medical_history:
# Patient Clinical Record

**Patient ID:** AUTO-GENERATED
**Name:** Sarah Thompson
**Age:** 45
**Gender:** Female

## Chief Complaint
Difficulty with balance and coordination following recovery from COVID-19.

## Medical History
- Post-COVID syndrome (long COVID, 8 months duration)
- Hypothyroidism (well-controlled)
- Anxiety disorder
- No history of falls prior to COVID infection

## Current Medications
- Levothyroxine 75mcg daily
- Escitalopram 10mg daily
- Vitamin D 2000 IU daily

## COVID-19 History
- Infected March 2025, moderate severity (no hospitalization)
- Persistent symptoms: fatigue, brain fog, dizziness, palpitations

## Physical Examination
- Height: 5'6" | Weight: 145 lbs | BMI: 23.4
- Orthostatic hypotension (BP drop 20 mmHg on standing)
- Tandem gait impaired
- Romberg test positive

## Gait Assessment
- Stride Length: 0.62 m
- Gait Speed: 1.05 m/s
- Symmetry Index: 0.88
- Gait appears normal but patient reports fatigue after 100 meters
- Difficulty with quick turns

## Recent Falls
- 2 falls in past 3 months (both when turning quickly)
- No injuries

## Work Status
Software engineer, working from home. Unable to return to office due to fatigue.

## Notes
Consider vestibular rehabilitation and dysautonomia workup.

PAT-811DAF72.name: Patient AF72
PAT-811DAF72.medical_history:
# Patient Clinical Record

**Patient ID:** AUTO-GENERATED
**Name:** William Davis
**Age:** 55
**Gender:** Male

## Chief Complaint
Pre-operative evaluation for elective knee replacement surgery.

## Medical History
- Severe osteoarthritis bilateral knees (right worse than left)
- Obesity
- Obstructive sleep apnea (on CPAP)
- Hyperlipidemia
- No cardiac history
- No diabetes

## Current Medications
- Atorvastatin 20mg daily
- Naproxen 500mg twice daily as needed
- Glucosamine supplement

## Surgical History
- Appendectomy (age 22)
- Right knee arthroscopy (2018)

## Physical Examination
- Height: 5'9" | Weight: 245 lbs | BMI: 36.2
- Bilateral knee crepitus
- Right knee ROM: 5-95 degrees (limited)
- Varus deformity right knee
- No edema

## Gait Assessment
- Stride Length: 0.55 m
- Gait Speed: 0.85 m/s
- Symmetry Index: 0.72
- Antalgic gait (right-sided limp)
- Uses cane for distances >100 meters

## Recent Falls
- No falls in past year
- Reports feeling stable despite knee pain

## Pre-Operative Goals
Assess fall risk for post-operative planning. Patient scheduled for right total knee arthroplasty in 4 weeks.

## Notes
Low baseline fall risk but will need fall prevention protocol post-surgery during rehabilitation phase.
